lesson.menu_desc: Reporting problems & getting help calmly -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Asking for Help & Emergencies
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Pricila Adamo | Aula 15 -- Asking for Help & Emergencies

listenings.text: Attention, please. This is a security announcement for all visitors. If you lose a personal item, such as a wallet, a passport, or a bag, please go to the help desk near the main entrance. Do not leave your luggage alone at any time. If you feel sick or need urgent help, look for a staff member in a blue uniform, or dial one-one-two from any phone. Our team is here to help you. Please stay calm, and speak slowly so we can understand you. Thank you, and enjoy your visit.

The first time I lost something abroad, I panicked. My wallet was gone, and I could only think in Portuguese. But then I learned one simple lesson: in an emergency, slow down. I take a deep breath, and I say three things clearly. What happened, where it happened, and what I need. Someone has taken my wallet. It happened near the station. I need to report it to the police. That is all. People want to help you, but first they need to understand you. Calm words travel further than fast words.

extra_audio.text: First, Pricila notices that her wallet is gone. Then she stays calm and finds a police officer. Next, she reports what happened and where. After that, the officer asks her to describe the wallet. Finally, she fills in a form and gets a copy of the report.
